title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary:
The reviewer wants four changes:

1. **Add `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive`** to `apt-get` commands in `images/keystone/Dockerfile` and `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile`, matching the fix already applied in `python-base`.
2. **Replace inline if/grep/PASS/FAIL logic** in `tests/container-images/verify_keystone.sh` with the shared `assert_contains` helper used everywhere else.
3. **Fix case-sensitive package matching** in `scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh` by adding the `/I` flag to `sed` (or normalizing to lowercase), since PyPI names are case-insensitive and mismatches cause silent duplicate constraints.
4. **Add a case-mismatch test** in `tests/scripts/test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh` to cover the case-insensitive matching fix and prevent regressions.

code_comments.body:
**`DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` missing â€” same gap as the `python-base` fix**

The `apt-get install` in Stage 2 installs `libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3`, which triggers Apache post-install helper scripts (`a2enmod`, debconf prompts). In some CI runner environments these can stall or emit unexpected interactive prompts when `DEBIAN_FRONTEND` is unset. The same root cause was identified and fixed in `images/python-base/Dockerfile` (line 11), but the fix was not propagated here.

Additionally, `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile` (line 11) has the same omission.

Both should follow the same pattern as `python-base`:

```suggestion
RUN DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3 \
    libldap-2.5-0 \
    libsasl2-2 \
    libxml2 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

And in `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile` line 11:
```dockerfile
RUN DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ...
```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive prefix to the apt-get RUN instruction in images/keystone/Dockerfile line 28. Also applied the same fix to images/venv-builder/Dockerfile line 11 as the reviewer requested, since it had the same omission.

code_comments.body:
**Inline if/grep pattern is inconsistent with `assert_contains` used elsewhere**

Test 4 duplicates the if/grep/PASS/FAIL idiom that `assertions.sh` was introduced to eliminate. Every other check in this file (and all other test scripts) uses the shared helpers. `assert_contains` from `tests/lib/assertions.sh` is a direct replacement:

```suggestion
test_runtime_apt_packages_installed() {
  echo "Test: runtime apt packages are installed"
  local dpkg_output
  dpkg_output=$(docker run --rm "$IMAGE" dpkg -l 2>&1)

  for pkg in libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3 libldap-2.5-0 libsasl2-2 libxml2; do
    assert_contains "$pkg is installed" "$dpkg_output" "$pkg"
  done
}
```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Replaced the inline if/grep/PASS/FAIL block in test_runtime_apt_packages_installed() with a single assert_contains call inside the for loop, matching the reviewer's suggested code exactly.

code_comments.body:
**Case-sensitive package matching can silently miss existing constraints**

The `sed` deletion pattern and the `cut -d'='` extraction are both case-sensitive (line 34 uses `"${line#-}"`, line 40 uses `cut -d'=' -f1`). PyPI package names are case-insensitive, and the OpenStack `upper-constraints.txt` mixes cases (e.g., `PyMySQL`, `oslo.config`).

If an override file writes `pymysql===2.1.0` (lowercase) but the constraint file contains `PyMySQL===2.0.0`, the `sed -i "/^pymysql===/d"` pattern will not match `PyMySQL===2.0.0`, so the old pin is left in place and the new line is appended â€” creating a duplicate with conflicting versions. Downstream `uv`/`pip` will then resolve against two conflicting constraints for the same package.

Consider adding case-insensitive matching to the `sed` delete:
```bash
sed -i "/^${escaped_package}===/Id" "$CONSTRAINTS"
```
(`/I` flag enables case-insensitive matching in GNU `sed`). Alternatively, normalise the package name to lowercase before constructing the pattern.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.

Line: 28-46

Comment:
**Case-sensitive package matching can silently miss existing constraints**

The `sed` deletion pattern and the `cut -d'='` extraction are both case-sensitive (line 34 uses `"${line#-}"`, line 40 uses `cut -d'=' -f1`). PyPI package names are case-insensitive, and the OpenStack `upper-constraints.txt` mixes cases (e.g., `PyMySQL`, `oslo.config`).

If an override file writes `pymysql===2.1.0` (lowercase) but the constraint file contains `PyMySQL===2.0.0`, the `sed -i "/^pymysql===/d"` pattern will not match `PyMySQL===2.0.0`, so the old pin is left in place and the new line is appended â€” creating a duplicate with conflicting versions. Downstream `uv`/`pip` will then resolve against two conflicting constraints for the same package.

Consider adding case-insensitive matching to the `sed` delete:
```bash
sed -i "/^${escaped_package}===/Id" "$CONSTRAINTS"
```
(`/I` flag enables case-insensitive matching in GNU `sed`). Alternatively, normalise the package name to lowercase before constructing the pattern.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added the /I flag (GNU sed case-insensitive matching) to both sed -i deletion patterns in apply-constraint-overrides.sh (lines 36 and 42), changing '/d' to '/Id' so that package name matching is case-insensitive.

code_comments.body:
**No case-sensitivity test â€” the case-mismatch bug described in `apply-constraint-overrides.sh` is undetected**

Test 2 and Test 5 use the same-cased package names in both the constraints file and the override file (e.g., `cryptography` in both). Since the script's `sed` pattern matching is case-sensitive, a test using a package whose canonical constraint name has different casing (e.g., `PyMySQL===2.0.0` in constraints, `pymysql===2.1.0` in override) would reveal the silent-duplicate failure. Consider adding a Test 7 (or extending Test 2) with a case-mismatched package to document the expected behaviour and prevent regressions once the fix is applied.

Since the script's `sed` pattern matching is case-sensitive, a test using a package whose canonical constraint name has different casing (e.g., `PyMySQL===2.0.0` in constraints, `pymysql===2.1.0` in override) would reveal the silent-duplicate failure. Consider adding a Test 7 (or extending Test 2) with a case-mismatched package to document the expected behaviour and prevent regressions once the fix is applied.

`````markdown
How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added Test 7 (test_case_insensitive_package_matching) to test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh that uses 'PyMySQL===1.1.0' in the constraints file and 'pymysql===1.1.1' (lowercase) in the override file, verifying the old mixed-case entry is removed and the new lowercase entry is present.

resolution_summary: All four review comments were addressed. DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive was added to both images/keystone/Dockerfile and images/venv-builder/Dockerfile (the reviewer noted the venv-builder gap as an additional location beyond the directly commented file). The inline if/grep pattern in verify_keystone.sh was replaced with the assert_contains helper. Case-insensitive sed matching (/Id flag) was added to both sed calls in apply-constraint-overrides.sh, and a new Test 7 was added to test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh to cover the case-mismatch scenario.
